title: 2 Terminology
body_text:
2	Terminology
2.1	Terminology
Client Centre
The Client Centre is a digital portal that provides secured access to Central 1âs applications and to information and news about our products and services.
Client Centre Users
Client Centre Users log in to the Client Centre to access certain applications. Client Centre Users canât manage CPS Admin Security Officers or other Client Centre Users.
CPS Admin
The Central Portal System (CPS) Admin application is used to add, edit, and delete Client Centre Users and CPS Admin Security Officers.
CPS Admin Security Officer
CPS Admin Security Officers use the CPS Admin or the User Management application to add, edit, and delete Client Centre Users. They can also delete and modify other CPS Admin Security Officers (with some exceptions) via the User Management application. CPS Admin Security Officers use the Client Centre to log in to certain applications.
File and Report Exchange
The File and Report Exchange allows authorized users to download files from Central 1 and upload files to Central 1. Users can connect to the File and Report Exchange via direct connection (SFTP) or online via the Client Centre.
FTP
File Transfer Protocol (FTP) is a standard network protocol used to transfer files from one computer to another.
Script
A script is an automated list of commands that are executed by a program or scripting engine. Scripts can be used to automate processes (e.g., downloading and uploading files to Central 1).
SFTP
Secure File Transfer Protocol (SFTP) is the method of connecting directly to the File and Report Exchange that uses a secure form of the FTP command.
User Management
User Management is an application used to add, edit, and delete Client Centre Users. It is also used to edit and delete other CPS Admin Security Officers and to manage 2-step security tokens. Eventually, User Management will replace CPS Admin.
END
